title: Extended JSON Web Token (EjwT): A Secure Authentication Framework with Dynamic Cryptographic Randomization and LSTM-based Anomaly Detection
abstract: JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) are widely adopted as part of stateless authentication systems, but they are still vulnerable to theft of tokens, brute-force attacks, 'alg none' attack risks, and unauthorized access due to the lack of encryption and server side revocation systems. In this paper, the Extended JSON Web Token (EjwT), a safe extension of JWTs based upon dynamic cryptographic randomization, is proposed. The main elements of the proposed scheme are nonce calculation, key identifier swapping, algorithm agility, and encryption using AES-GCM, together with real-time anomaly detection performed by Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. This work trained the model with a dataset containing more than 33 million authentication events. Techniques like Interquartile Range (IQR) outlier filtering, SMOTE to handle class imbalance, and Pearson Correlation Coefficient driven feature selection are also employed. And with empirical validation, a 99.60% accuracy rate, 95.10% precision, 97.00% recall rate, and 96.04% F1-score are achieved, clearly offering a great step forward from traditional JWTs with regard to integrity, confidentiality, revocation, and resilience to brute force attacks with multi-layered risk-based authentication.
conferenceName: 5th International Conference on Electrical, Computer & Telecommunication Engineering (ICECTE 2026)

title: THz Metamaterial Absorber with KNN-Based Performance Prediction for Electromagnetic Interference Shielding and Sensing Applications
abstract: Metamaterials are synthetically engineered materials that reflect unique properties attributable to their geometric configuration. The conventional design of MMA is a complex process that necessitates the utilization of computationally demanding full-wave simulators. This research presents a THz metamaterial absorber (MMA) comprising a metallic resonant patch situated above a metallic ground plane, with an FR-4 dielectric substrate in between them. The MMA achieves a sharp resonant peak, a Q-factor of 23, and an absorptivity of 99.96% at 0.89 THz, with a sensitivity of 1.59(905GHz/RIU). At the minimum working frequency, the proposed design is thin (0.027λ) and compact (0.38λ). The KNN regressor attains an RMSE of 0.4576, an adjusted R2 value of 0.9993 for optimal absorptivity prediction, and yields an 85% reduction in simulation duration. It is suitable for electromagnetic interference shielding, stealth, and sensing applications, ensuring reliable performance across diverse environments.
conferenceName: 6th IEEE International Conference on Telecommunications and Photonics (ICTP) 2025

title: MachDishari: IoT- Enabled Aquaculture Using Federated Machine Learning and XAI
abstract: The integration of innovative technologies such as the Internet of Things (IoT) and Machine Learning (ML) presents a transformative approach to enhancing traditional fish farming practices. Conventional fish farming often faces challenges due to high labor costs, unmonitored water quality monitoring changes, fish disease outbreaks, and recommendation of optimal fish species. In this paper, present MachDishari system that is IoT based ML recommendation system designed to automate water quality monitoring and suggests appropriate fish species based on various water quality parameters —including pH, temperature, TDS, water level, DO, BOD, COD and turbidity. We developed IoT sensors for real time data collection of water parameters, and machine learning algorithms including Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN) and Logistic Regression (LR) were applied to the pond water real-time dataset. Federated Learning (FL) into our design integrate for sensitive data privacy and train machine learning models collaboratively without centralizing sensitive data. This approach preserves data privacy and enables farms in remote areas to collaboratively improve model performance without risking data leakage. A range of XAI methods was applied in the present study to enhance the interpretability and transparency of fish recommendation. Among tested models XGBoost that achieved the best performance, delivering 99.8% accuracy was deployed in a web interface and app, enabling farmers to receive real time water quality assessments and fish species recommendations and then expected to enhance fish yield for modern aquaculture with preserving data privacy.
conferenceName: 4th IEEE International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-intelligence and Internet-of-Things
